top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on paved high-altitude lakeside highways, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
56ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride's paved high-altitude lakeside highways forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 72 miles.
72 miles on paved high-altitude lakeside highways burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
At America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Varied gradients on paved high-altitude lakeside highways demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,024ft of climbing on paved high-altitude lakeside highways does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved high-altitude lakeside highways. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride’s 72 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 72 miles of paved high-altitude lakeside highways in Stateline, Nevada, USA.
